appeal_letter:
To the Appeals Department,

I am appealing the denial of coverage for outpatient physical therapy services performed on 05/01/2026.

The denial states that coverage is refused because the services exceed the authorized limit and that prior authorization for additional visits was not obtained. Specifically, the letter explains that my plan document limits outpatient physical therapy to 20 visits per calendar year without medical necessity review, that the 20 authorized visits have been used, and that because my provider did not submit a prior authorization request demonstrating medical necessity before continuing treatment, the claims were denied.

I want to address that exact ground directly. The plan permits outpatient physical therapy beyond 20 visits when additional visits are supported by a medical necessity review. My clinical situation is precisely the kind of individualized circumstance that review is meant to capture. I underwent a total knee replacement and then experienced a minor post-operative infection that delayed my rehabilitation progress. As a result of that complication, I continue to have significant mobility deficits, and the additional physical therapy was a continuation of medically necessary care for that same episode.

I recognize that a prior authorization request for the additional visits was not submitted before treatment continued. The physical therapy clinic assumed that the initial authorization covered the entire episode of care and did not request an extension after the twentieth visit. That was an administrative oversight in the request process, not an absence of medical necessity. The underlying clinical facts, the post-operative infection, the delayed recovery, and my ongoing mobility deficits, are the facts a medical necessity review would consider, and I ask that the review be conducted now on those facts. I therefore request that the additional visits be authorized and the claims paid, with the medical necessity of the continued care evaluated on its merits.

I also note that the denial letter does not state the deadline by which I must appeal, the process for submitting an appeal, or my right to a full and fair review of this decision. I assert my right to a complete and fair review, and I ask that you provide the applicable appeal deadline and the steps for any further levels of review.

Please reconsider this denial and authorize the additional outpatient physical therapy visits and the associated claims. I am prepared to provide any further documentation needed to support this request.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: A copy of the plan document language describing the medical necessity review process for outpatient physical therapy beyond 20 visits
The surgeon's operative report for the total knee replacement and documentation of the post-operative infection and its treatment
The physical therapist's progress notes and current functional assessment documenting the ongoing mobility deficits
A letter of medical necessity from the treating provider supporting continued physical therapy for this episode of care
The dates and visit records for the physical therapy services at issue, including the visit performed on 05/01/2026
Any written authorization or communication showing the scope and limits of the original physical therapy authorization
The plan's appeal deadline, appeal submission instructions, and description of the right to a full and fair review
